steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks how Cummings and Knapp contributed to Lullfitz's research. The notes show they confirmed their ancestors carried youlk when traveling — a concrete detail that supported Lullfitz's hypothesis about the plant's distribution.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Lullfitz found that Cummings and Knapp had information relevant to her study of *Platysace deflexa*'s distribution.": identifies who Cummings and Knapp are without explaining what specific information they provided. ✗
  • "Cummings and Knapp confirmed that DNA sequences from the *Platysace deflexa* plants Lullfitz had gathered were in fact very similar.": credits Cummings and Knapp with conclusions that belong to Lullfitz herself. ✗
  • "Lullfitz consulted Cummings and Knapp, members of the Noongar Aboriginal group, who assisted with her study of *Platysace deflexa*, a small shrub with edible tubers.": tells us about their background rather than what they contributed to the study. ✗
  • "By indicating that their ancestors transported *Platysace deflexa*, Cummings and Knapp provided information that supported Lullfitz's hypothesis.": states the specific information they gave (ancestors carried youlk) and explains why it mattered to Lullfitz's research. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
